findings.description: DIRECTIVE-20260505-004 fulfilled — PROP-020 authored covering all 10 deliverable structure sections.
findings.details: Schema concrete with 3 example entries (verdict_changed WIN-022, new_item WIN-072, pre-tldr-schema large_rewrite WIN-050). All 5 open design questions answered with reasoning. Both integrity prompt edit (§7g new section) and curmudgeon prompt edit (Steps 1-4 replacement) drafted with before/after. Fallback contract specified for missing/malformed/stale/sha-mismatch states. OBE-skip cap of 3 argued with bump-to-5 escape hatch. Runnable Node script skeleton included (compute-drift-audit.js, ~140 lines). Did NOT modify the four target files (monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md, monitor/prompts/reference/curmudgeon-change-and-holistic.md, monitor/prompts/structure-integrity.md, monitor/scripts/compute-drift-audit.js) per the directive's do_not_implement clause.

findings.description: Key choices summary for operator review.
findings.details: Path: monitor/integrity/drift-audit.json (writer's directory). Cap: top-30 large_rewrite + tldr_only, uncapped verdict_changed + new_item. Cadence: 72h gate, absorbed into integrity's daily 9am run. OBE-cap: 3. v1 scope: WIN-NNN only (sections deferred to Phase 2 once a canonical section fingerprint shape is standardized). Fallback: drift-audit.json missing/malformed/>168h-old/sha-mismatch all route to in-prompt scan. All choices match operator's stated leans.

findings.description: v1 PROP-020 scope is WIN-only; sections are not scored.
findings.details: Empirical justification: of 248 distinct review items, only ~69 WIN-NNN items carry a canonical comparable fingerprint (40 pre-tldr + 15 post-tldr + 14 with bespoke shape). Section reviews (part1...part10), EXP-NNN, ISS-NNN, HOLISTIC-* all have bespoke fingerprint shapes that cannot be uniformly compared. Phase 2 extension required for sections — separate PROP. Items without comparable fingerprints (179 of 248) are surfaced in stats.items_skipped_no_comparable_fingerprint for integrity's daily report.
